quote: Huang Zheng IPO letter (June 30, 2018): 'It will be a Costco and Disney driven by distributed intelligent agent networks rather than centralized AI systems.'
PDD launched Xinpinmu on March 25, 2026. Initial RMB 15B cash injection. Two Shanghai entities: 上海新拼姆鸿乔 (RMB 10B) and 上海新拼姆朴动 (RMB 5B). Three-year plan: RMB 100B total.
Co-CEO Zhao Jiazhen framed Xinpinmu as 'recreating another Pinduoduo' — 3-year plan to build self-operated brands for global markets via Temu's 90+ country network.

Duan Yongping on Xueqiu: '这个其实就是Costco的样子。拼多多如果线上品牌只有4000个左右的SKU的话，10年后会是相当厉害的。' ('This is essentially the Costco model. If PDD's online brand has around 4,000 SKUs, in 10 years it will be quite formidable.')

Duan Yongping: '拼多多不需要抢客户生意，只要扶植爆品公司就好，监督质量，达到标准就可以把自己的品牌授权给厂家。' ('PDD doesn't need to steal merchants' business — just nurture hit-product companies, supervise quality, and license its brand to qualified factories.')

JD self-operated (自营) model: JD buys inventory from existing brands (Samsung, Nike, P&G) and resells. JD is a distributor with 14-19% gross margins. JD Jingzao is a tiny side project with undisclosed revenue.
Amazon Basics private-label model: Amazon created products competing directly with 3P sellers, then retreated under FTC antitrust pressure. Cut from 45 brands to <20. FTC trial October 2026.
Xinpinmu = Costco curated model + PDD traffic-matching efficiency + Made in China supply chain responsiveness. Brand creation (like Kirkland), not inventory distribution (like JD).

JD Jingzao covers 600+ categories but revenue NOT separately disclosed. JD explicitly says 'not seeking brand premium, compete on supply chain efficiency.' Tiny side project vs core JD 1P distributor business.
Anker Innovations: 44.6% gross margin (9M2025), 9.38% operating margin. FY2024 revenue RMB 24.71B (+41% YoY). TTM revenue RMB 29.28B. Founded 2011, $9.4B market cap.
UGREEN: 37.4% gross margin FY2024, ~8.7% operating margin. Revenue RMB 6.17B (+28.5% YoY). Chinese consumer electronics brand-owner benchmark.
Xiaomi IoT/Lifestyle: 23-25% gross margin. Q3 2025 overall gross margin 22.9% (record). IoT annual revenue RMB 123.2B (+18.3%). Deliberately cheap, high volume model.
Kirkland: ~15% markup cap, 5-8pp higher margins than national brands. 23% of Costco revenue from 12.5% of SKU assortment. $58-90B annual sales.
By Q1 2023, PDD shifted positioning from '多实惠+多乐趣' (multi-value + multi-fun) to '多实惠+好服务' (multi-value + good service), deprioritizing the Disney component.
PDD C2M evolution — Phase 1 (2018): New Brand Plan 1.0, 1,000 factory partners. Phase 2 (2020): 2.0 upgrade, 5,000 partners, RMB 1T target. Phase 3 (2022-25): Temu + 新质供给. Phase 4 (2026): Xinpinmu.
Key shift in Phase 4: from 'helping factories build their brands' to 'owning the brand and licensing it to factories.' PDD becomes the brand owner, factory becomes contract manufacturer.
新质供给 program teams visited 100+ manufacturing clusters (义乌美妆, 深圳数码, 山东零食, 邵东箱包, 平湖羽绒, 威海钓具). Results: average order value +47%, repeat purchase rate from 23% to 40%.
PDD FY2025 revenue RMB 431.8B (+10% YoY), down from 131% growth in Q1 2024. Revenue growth deceleration is the sharpest in Chinese e-commerce history.
PDD domestic MAU at 872M, only +3M over two years. Organic user ceiling reached for domestic platform.
US de minimis permanently revoked May 2, 2025 for China/HK. $100 per-parcel postal fee on every shipment. Low-value parcel volume dropped 85% (4M daily to 600K).
PDD cash + short-term investments: RMB 422.3B ($60.4B), +27% YoY. 56% of market cap is cash. Zero capital return to shareholders.
FY2025 costs grew +23% vs revenue +10%. R&D expense +30% YoY. Cost structure shifting toward reinvestment.
Zhao Jiazhen became co-CEO in Dec 2025 governance restructure, with explicit mandate to 'rebuild PDD in 3 years' through Xinpinmu.
New Chinese regulations (effective Oct 2025, Apr 2026) ban platform price coercion and anti-competitive self-preferencing.
Kirkland Signature: $58-90B annual sales from ~600 SKUs, representing 23% of Costco revenue from just 12.5% of SKU assortment.

Kirkland supplier model: Costco contracts with premium national-brand manufacturers. Starbucks roasts Kirkland coffee. Duracell makes Kirkland batteries. Factory produces for BOTH national brand AND Kirkland simultaneously.
Costco CEO personally signs off on each new Kirkland item. Products must match or exceed national brand quality at 20% lower price. Categories where national brands can't be matched are abandoned.
Costco caps markup at 14% for national brands, 15% for Kirkland. Margin premium comes from cutting out brand marketing costs, not charging more.
Costco 1983-1993: spent first decade refining 4,000 SKUs rather than rushing expansion. Compressed costs, stabilized quality, built trust. Membership fee model aligns incentives.
Costco ROIC: 33.7% vs Walmart's 17.1%. Revenue per employee: $850K vs $348K industry average.

4,000 SKUs covers approximately 80% of high-frequency household consumption needs, creating a consumption closed loop. Shifts user behavior from multi-platform price comparison to single-platform trust.
Per-SKU authorization model: PDD handles standardized products (标品) under Xinpinmu brand; merchants handle non-standard, personalized, long-tail products (非标). Structural division of labor.
Even Kirkland displaces some national brands: Kind bars, Planters nuts, Pampers lost shelf space. Lululemon lawsuit in 2025 over Kirkland lookalike. Dual-manufacturing model is not zero-conflict.
Sina Finance warns: 'PDD的中小型商家将受到最大冲击' — small/medium merchants face biggest impact on standardized product categories.
Merchant conflict risk: PDD will be both platform operator (setting rules) and brand competitor (selling products). 'Referee and athlete' concern raised explicitly.
PDD's traffic algorithm could preference Xinpinmu products in search results — same antitrust concern that triggered FTC suit against Amazon.

Landed cost: OLD MODEL $10 product = $10 + $7 air freight + $5.40 tariff (54%) + $100 per-parcel fee = ~$122.40. NEW MODEL = $10 + $0.50 ocean + $2.50 tariff (25%) + $3 warehouse = ~$16. Per-parcel fee is the killer.
EU flat EUR 3 customs duty per item on all sub-EUR 150 parcels from July 1, 2026. EUR 2 handling fee from Nov 2026. Full standard tariffs from 2028. 4.6B shipments/year affected, 91% from China.
Air freight: $6-10/parcel consolidated. Ocean FCL: $0.10-0.30/kg for dense cargo. Air freight is 4-6x more expensive than ocean per unit weight.
Temu 13 self-owned warehouses globally (10 EU, 2 US, 1 Mexico). EU target: 80% local fulfillment by 2026.
Temu reports bulk shipping to UK warehouses reduced per-item logistics costs by 40-60% vs air freight, while improving delivery from weeks to days.
Xinpinmu adds costs PDD never bore: inventory risk, working capital, brand development, QC infrastructure. Chinese media: '搬砖类型的苦活累活' (heavy brick-carrying work), departure from asset-light DNA.
Xinpinmu full-stack export support: product standards, warehousing logistics, IP protection, legal assistance, regulatory compliance for global brand launches.
Private label brands deliver 25-30% higher gross margins vs generic. Higher ASP dilutes tariff as % of value. Over 54% of private label sellers report 20%+ profit margins.
Scenario analysis: Bull (30% GM, RMB 50B rev) = RMB 5B op profit. Base (22% GM, RMB 30B) = RMB 1.5B. Bear (15% GM, RMB 15B) = breakeven. Accretive at ~22% GM with RMB 25B+ revenue.
PDD EV ~$85B. Net income $15.4B = 5.6x EV/NI. Cash RMB 422B = 56% of market cap. Deep value multiple.
Option value: If Xinpinmu generates $3-5B annual operating profit, worth $30-75B at 10-15x. Represents 35-86% upside to current EV. Currently priced at zero.
PDD stock +4.61% on March 25, 2026 (announcement day) despite FY2025 net income declining 12% — market reacted positively to Xinpinmu vision.
Nomura upgraded PDD to Buy, $136 target. Called PDD 'too cheap at 8.6x forward P/E.' Xinpinmu helps Temu cope with regulatory scrutiny and tariff barriers.
Goldman Sachs: Buy, $147 target on PDD. JPMorgan: Hold, $125. BofA: $170. CMBI: $187.9.
Analyst consensus: 26 Buy, 0 Sell, average price target $137-148. All major brokers maintain Buy except JPM at Hold.
Li Lu / Himalaya Capital: PDD at 14.64% of $3.57B portfolio (4th largest position). Held through Q4 2025 decline without selling. Initiated Q2 2025.
Duan Yongping / H&H International: increased PDD by ~34.55% in Q4 2025, adding ~3M shares during decline. 7.75M total shares, $906M position. PDD founder's angel investor and mentor.

Duan Yongping: Costco 1983-1993 spent first decade polishing 4,000 SKUs. '10 years is not about time itself but emphasizing that quality business models need time to be forged.' Management says 3, Duan says 10.
